[{"role": "system", "content": "# TASK
1. Analyze the provided labeled examples to identify the observable visual features that distinguish each class.
2. Examine the Target Image and compare it strictly against the provided examples.
3. Determine which label from the provided options best matches the Target Image.

# INSTRUCTIONS
- Base your decision only on observable visual evidence in the examples and the Target Image.
- Use the examples to infer discriminative visual patterns for each class.
- Do not use external world knowledge, hidden assumptions, or speculative attributes.
- Use only labels from the provided final options list.
- Choose exactly one final label.
- Output only the requested XML tags.
- Do not output any text outside the XML tags.
- Follow the condition-specific XML blocks first, then output exactly one final <response> block.
- The final line of the entire answer must be <response>LABEL</response>.
- Never omit the final <response> block, even when another explanation block is required.

Represent the classification evidence using minimal and concise Description Logic statements.

# WHAT TO WRITE
- In <tbox>, define the formal class conditions (Necessary, Sufficient, or Necessary & Sufficient) using Description Logic (DL) axioms derived from the labeled examples to describe which visual features are associated with a class.
- In <abox>, state only the specific property assertions observed in the Target Image to record which visual features are present.
- In <dl_explanation>, explain exactly how the specific assertions in the ABox satisfy the formal axioms in the TBox to correctly classify the Target Image.

# HOW TO INTERPRET THE STATEMENTS
- [Class] sqsubseteq hasVisualFeature([Feature]) means: images of this class tend to have this visual feature in the provided examples (necessary condition)
- hasVisualFeature([Feature]) sqsubseteq [Class] means: observing this visual feature is enough to classify the image as from this class in the provided examples (sufficient condition)
- [Class] equiv hasVisualFeature([F1]) and hasVisualFeature([F2]) means: this combination of features defines this class in the provided examples (necessary and sufficient condition)

# RULES
- Use only observable visual features.
- Do not use hidden properties, external knowledge, or speculation.
- Treat all axioms as visually grounded in the provided examples.
- Keep the output concise.

<tbox>
- [Class] sqsubseteq hasVisualFeature([F])
- hasVisualFeature([F]) sqsubseteq [Class]
- [Class] equiv hasVisualFeature([F1]) and hasVisualFeature([F2])
</tbox>
<abox>
- hasVisualFeature(Target, [F])
- hasVisualFeature(Target, [F])
</abox>
<dl_explanation>
- The Target Image contains the properties asserted in the ABox.
- Explain how these ABox assertions satisfy the TBox axioms.
- Conclude why [Class] is the logically derived label based on that explanation.
</dl_explanation>

raw_response_text:
```xml
<tbox>
    <!-- Class 45 (Orange flowers with red spots, tubular shape, clustered blooms) -->
    [Class_45] sqsubseteq hasVisualFeature([bright_orange_petals]) and
                      hasVisualFeature([red_spots_or_marks]) and
                      hasVisualFeature([tubular_or_funnel_shaped_flowers]) and
                      hasVisualFeature([clustered_blooms_on_stem]).

    <!-- Class 44 (Purple flowers, broad petals, slightly ruffled edges) -->
    [Class_44] sqsubseteq hasVisualFeature([deep_purple_petals]) and
                      hasVisualFeature([broad_flat_petals]) and
                      hasVisualFeature([ruffled_or_wavy_petals]) and
                      hasVisualFeature([central_yellow_or_brown_stamen]).

    <!-- Class 77 (Large white/pink petals, layered/ruffled edges, prominent yellow center) -->
    [Class_77] equiv hasVisualFeature([large_white_or_pink_petals]) and
                       hasVisualFeature([layered_or_ruffled_petals]) and
                       hasVisualFeature([prominent_yellow_center]) and
                       hasVisualFeature([broad_flat_petal_shape]).
</tbox>

<abox>
    <!-- Target Image Observations -->
    hasVisualFeature(Target, [bright_orange_petals]).
    hasVisualFeature(Target, [red_spots_or_marks]).
    hasVisualFeature(Target, [tubular_or_funnel_shaped_flowers]).
    hasVisualFeature(Target, [clustered_blooms_on_stem]).
</abox>

<dl_explanation>
    The Target Image exhibits bright orange petals with red spots, tubular/funnel-shaped flowers, and clustered blooms on a stem. These features directly match the necessary and sufficient conditions for [Class_45] as defined in the TBox. The presence of these visual features aligns with the examples of Class 45, which also exhibit these characteristics. The other classes (44 and 77) do not match these features, as Class 44 is characterized by deep purple petals with broad flat shapes and ruffled edges, and Class 77 by large white/pink petals with layered ruffles and a prominent yellow center.
</dl_explanation>

<response>45</response>
```

raw_response_text:
```xml
<tbox>
    <!-- Class 44 (Purple flowers with multiple petals, slightly ruffled edges, yellow/green center) -->
    [Class_44] sqsubseteq hasVisualFeature([deep_purple_color]) and
                     hasVisualFeature([multiple_petals]) and
                     hasVisualFeature([ruffled_petals]) and
                     hasVisualFeature([yellow_green_center]).

    <!-- Class 45 (Bright orange/yellow flowers, tubular shape, red spots) -->
    [Class_45] sqsubseteq hasVisualFeature([bright_orange_yellow_color]) and
                     hasVisualFeature([tubular_petals]) and
                     hasVisualFeature([red_spots]).

    <!-- Class 77 (Large white/pink flowers, broad petals, smooth edges, yellow center) -->
    [Class_77] sqsubseteq hasVisualFeature([large_white_pink_color]) and
                     hasVisualFeature([broad_petals]) and
                     hasVisualFeature([smooth_petals]) and
                     hasVisualFeature([yellow_center]).
</tbox>

<abox>
    hasVisualFeature(Target, [deep_purple_color]).
    hasVisualFeature(Target, [multiple_petals]).
    hasVisualFeature(Target, [ruffled_petals]).
    hasVisualFeature(Target, [yellow_green_center]).
</abox>

<dl_explanation>
    The Target Image exhibits the following observable visual features:
    - Deep purple coloration (matches [deep_purple_color]).
    - Multiple petals (matches [multiple_petals]).
    - Ruffled petal edges (matches [ruffled_petals]).
    - A yellow/green center (matches [yellow_green_center]).

    These features align with the TBox axioms for [Class_44], which requires all four properties to be present. The Target Image does not match the bright orange/yellow tubular flowers with red spots (Class 45) nor the large white/pink broad-petaled flowers with smooth edges (Class 77). Therefore, the Target Image satisfies the necessary and sufficient conditions for [Class_44].
</dl_explanation>

<response>44</response>
```
